texts.full_text: ” opens it with his keyring, takes a swig and struts around the gathering. The way he holds the bottle when he walks, where he rests the bottle where he sits andthe way hebrings the bottle to his mouth is all acarefully acted show.He needsto lookcool, suave, sophisticated.Poser! Swillers of NASCAR beers sneer at a BeerSamurai pouring his favorite libation into a glass. They have no idea that there’s a reason for this act andthat reason is that our beer tastes good not because we thinkit makes uslook cool. In my opinion it does sh...taste better in theproper vessel of choice! As I wrote above, toomanytimes on summer days or evenings at outdoor gatherings (e.,g,, cookouts),one sees so-called “men” grab a cold bottle from an ice chest and popoff its cap with their trusty keychain opener. They thenstroll aroundholding itby the neckas if to show everyone howmanly they are, that this is THEIR beer of choice when awayat work or play…likenothingelse will do for them but only THAT particular brand/type (and maybe anotherone from same company). Thiswhole actseems completely planned out beforehand: How he holdsit while walking; where does itsbottom end rest upon sitting down again?Everything about this performancescreams 'posing.' Then there are thosewho drink beers associated with NASCAR. Fisher III > Ralph E. Fisher III's (luxuryhomesofstlouis) Blog 4 Tips for Buying St Louis Homes & Condo’s for Sale By Ralph E. Fisher III Real Estate Agent with The Fisher Group / ReMax Results Email Short URL Share: July 06, 2010 02:35 PM We, meaning the real estate industry, say, "It's a buyer's market!" Buyers think that means that deals are available out in the big wide world of St Louis homes and condo's for sale. Although that's true, it's not the whole truth. The whole truth is that money pits, bad deals and half-hearted sellers still exist. Without careful consideration, you may not get the deal you expected. If you're in the market for one of the St. Louis or St. Charles homes or condo's for sale, follow these tips to 4 Tips For Buying a Home: 1) Do your research and thoroughly investigate any property before making an offer; this includes checking out comparable sales data as well local crime statistics/neighborhood conditions (e..g., proximity of schools). 2.) Get pre-approved for financing so you know exactly how much money can spend on buying the house without having worry about getting denied later down line when it comes time close deal. 3) Have an experienced real estate
